# From Feedback to Roadmap: The Full Product Loop (Userback, Dec 2025 / Apr 2026)

## Summary
Userback's own blog post (published December 2025, updated April 2026) explicitly describes the full feedback-to-roadmap loop: Feature Portal for idea collection and voting → status transitions → public roadmap → voter notifications on "Shipped" → NPS/CSAT micro-surveys. Also introduces the hybrid public/private/restricted roadmap strategy. This is the single clearest first-party source describing Userback's complete workflow.

## Key quotations / statistics
- Canonical status model: `Under Review → Planned → In Progress → Shipped`
- "When you mark an item as 'Shipped' in Userback, you have the option to send a notification to everyone who voted for or commented on that idea. But don't just say 'It's done.' Use this moment to drive adoption."
- "In Userback, the Roadmap isn't a separate entity. It's a visual extension of your Feature Portal. Every item starts as an Idea in the Portal. As you progress its status... it automatically populates your Roadmap."
- **Hybrid roadmap strategy:**
  1. Public Roadmap: High-level themes and "committed" features
  2. Private Roadmap: Detailed internal planning, tech debt, "maybe" features
  3. Restricted Access: Limit roadmap to authenticated users only
- "Not every roadmap needs to be public. Total transparency can sometimes backfire if competitors are watching."
- Micro-survey types: NPS, CSAT, micro-surveys to validate roadmap ideas before committing
- "Userback is a complete product management platform. Beyond collecting feedback, it helps you prioritize what to build, communicate your roadmap, and close the loop with users when their ideas ship."

## Annotations for stinger-forge
- **Most useful Userback source** - covers the full loop end-to-end with first-party authority.
- The hybrid roadmap (public + private + authenticated-restricted) is a key point for `guides/05-public-roadmap-playbook.md`.
- The "roadmap as visual extension of portal, not separate entity" framing is a critical design principle that differentiates Userback from tools where roadmap and feedback board are separate.
- Voter notification text advice ("don't just say it's done, drive adoption") is a practical tip for `guides/03-status-transition-policy.md`.
- The micro-survey-to-validate-roadmap-ideas pattern is useful for the collection surface taxonomy and bridges into discovery work.
